framework: A path qualifies only when >=3 prominent, proven people in the field visibly share the capability, each with a resolving link — the pattern across standouts is the evidence, not market demand. Ranked by strength of that pattern. Curated editorial, not DOL-derived.

paths.path: Run a public chemistry video channel
paths.what_it_is: Film and narrate real syntheses and reactions for a public YouTube or TikTok channel, explaining the chemistry as you go, consistently enough to build a lasting audience.
paths.why_it_signals: It proves your lab technique and your explanations both hold up under public scrutiny — verifiable skill an employer can check before ever meeting you.

paths.what_it_is: Get a real feature, fix, or set of docs merged into widely used chemistry software such as RDKit, or build and publicly document your own cheminformatics tool.
paths.why_it_signals: A merged contribution to software the field runs on is public proof you can turn chemistry judgment into working code, not just describe it.

paths.path: Write publicly about industry and drug-discovery chemistry
paths.what_it_is: Run a long-running blog or newsletter that translates real pharma or chemical-industry work, technical judgment, and trends for a broad technical readership.
paths.why_it_signals: Sustained, well-argued industry writing proves domain fluency plus the rarer ability to explain it clearly — evidence of judgment beyond your years of experience.
